_comment: P37 EXAMPLE jurisdictional-overlay records (schema demonstration; replaced per deployment). Optional bucket, loaded only when creator-os-config jurisdictional_overlay is on. Advisory planning information only, never a legal or permitting determination. Each record is cache-indexed via its 'text' field; tools/geo_overlay.py evaluates it. Geometry here is a tiny illustrative box, not a real boundary.
title: Example historic-preservation district overlay (geometry kind)
text: Example geometry overlay: a local historic-preservation district that triggers design review (matching historic frame profiles) when a project point falls inside the district boundary. Real boundaries are pulled from a municipal ArcGIS open-data layer in EPSG:4326 and cached simplified; this record carries an illustrative box only. Point-in-polygon is evaluated offline in tools/geo_overlay.py.

title: Example High-Velocity Hurricane Zone overlay (attribute kind)
text: Example attribute overlay: the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone applies to all of Miami-Dade and Broward counties by statute, so it is an attribute test on county FIPS (12086 or 12011), not a polygon. HVHZ triggers impact-rated products and Miami-Dade NOA/TAS protocols. Evaluated offline against the project's county FIPS.

title: Example sea-level-rise projection overlay (versioned-fact kind)
text: Example versioned-fact overlay: a regional sea-level-rise projection used to set additional resilience freeboard. It is a dated numeric value tied to its source edition, not a boundary; it is superseded when a new projection edition is published. Freeboard itself is imposed by local floodplain ordinance.
